data.title: Inomin Mines announces non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Inomin Mines (TSXV:MINE:CA) announced on Thursday a non-brokered private placement of securities to raise total gross proceeds up to $200,000
  • The financing will consist of non-flow-through units (the "NFT Units") to be sold at a price of $0.035 per NFT Unit, said the company. 
  • "Each NFT unit will consist of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant. Each Warrant will entitle the holder thereof to purchase one additional share for a period of two years from the date of issuance at an exercise price of $0.05 per share," stated the company, adding that,  "a maximum of 10 million NFT units will be issued in the financing."
  • Inomin intends to allocate the gross proceeds raised from the sale of the NFT Units as follows: 50% for general working capital; 35% to pay non-arm's length management and geological service fees; 10% for investor relations activities; and 5% for property evaluations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Supreme Court preserves Fed independence, lets Trump shake up other agencies
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court on Thursday blocked President Donald Trump from removing Federal Reserve officials but cleared the way for firings at other independent federal agencies, according to a filing.

The Fed is a  "uniquely structured, quasi-private entity" that follows in the distinct historical tradition of the First and Second Banks of the United States," the court said in the Trump v. Wilcox document.

The decision comes as Trump has previously brought up the possibility of dismissing Fed Chair Jerome Powell, an unfavorable suggestion among market participants.

But Trump still got the green light to fire officials at other independent federal agencies. 

The high court's liberal justices opposed the ruling that permitted the Trump layoffs.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Goodyear Tire & Rubber sells majority of chemical business
data.description:
• The Goodyear Tire & Rubber (NASDAQ:GT) has sold a majority of its chemical business to an affiliate of private equity firm, Gemspring Capital Management, for $650M.
  • The acquisition includes two facilities in Houston and Beaumont, Texas, and an R&D facility in Akron, Ohio.
  • Goodyear (NASDAQ:GT) will retail its chemical facilities in Niagara Falls, NY and Bayport, Texas and rights to all products produced at these facilities.
  • The transaction is expected to close in late 2025.
  • The transaction is in line with the company’s Goodyear Forward program to achieve record quarterly savings and at least $2B in asset sales.
  • “With the sale of our chemical business, we continue to demonstrate our commitment to optimizing our portfolio and creating shareholder value,” said Goodyear CEO Mark Stewart.
  • SEC filing

data.title: Thermon outlines $495M–$535M revenue target for fiscal 2026 amid tariff headwinds and diversified market growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Thermon Group Holdings, Inc. (THR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bruce Thames highlighted that "the fourth quarter was another period of solid execution by our team, which resulted in further strength in our OpEx recurring revenues, continued bookings momentum, and strong margin expansion." Thames reported 3% organic growth, the first in over a year, and noted significant improvement in order trends across verticals, especially LNG, where five major awards have been secured. The backlog increased 29% from last year, with organic backlog up 20% due to momentum in diversified verticals and a rebound in oil and gas markets.
  • Thames stated, "our strict financial discipline and improved operating profitability enabled us to finish fiscal 2025 in a strong financial position with net leverage of just under 1 times" and announced a refresh of the share repurchase authorization back to $50 million.
  • The R&D team announced 28 new product and software releases, while the recent acquisition of Vapor Power expanded the addressable market and sales pipeline by 25%. Thames emphasized the impact of operational excellence initiatives, with EBITDA margin reaching 22.7% for the quarter, a 423 basis point improvement year-over-year.
  • CFO Jan Schott reported, "Revenue in the fourth quarter was $134.1 million, a year-over-year increase of 5%, driven by continued momentum in OpEx revenues, including solid growth at Vapor Power and contribution from F.A.T.I." Schott also noted a book-to-bill of 1.04 times and total liquidity of $137 million.

Outlook

  • Thames provided fiscal 2026 guidance: "revenue in a range of $495 million to $535 million, representing 3.5% growth at the midpoint of the range. Adjusted EBITDA is in a range of $104 million to $114 million, essentially flat at the midpoint of the range." Margin headwinds are expected in the first half due to tariffs, with price increases anticipated to offset these impacts later in the year. Thames stated, "the current guidance contemplates slowing growth in the second half of the fiscal year."

Financial Results

  • Schott detailed that OpEx revenues were $111.8 million in the fourth quarter, up 7% from the previous year and representing 83% of total revenues for the quarter. Large project revenue was $22.3 million, down 5% year-over-year but up 20% from the previous quarter.
  • Orders increased 19% on a reported basis and nearly 14% organically, with notable strength in chemical, petrochemical, and rail and transit markets. EMEA revenue was up 51%, Canada sales declined 6%, and APAC revenues were $9.2 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $30.5 million for the quarter, up from $23.6 million last year. Free cash flow for fiscal 2025 was $52.9 million. Share repurchases totaled $14 million in the quarter, with $14.5 million in debt repaid.

Q&A

  • Chip Moore, ROTH Capital Partners: Asked about LNG resurgence and margin cadence. Bruce Thames explained, "Since the lift of the moratorium in the January timeframe, there was always really a number of projects that were in the queue in our pipeline, and we've seen those move forward pretty quickly." On margin, Thames described near-term headwinds from tariffs with mitigation expected in the second half.
  • Brian Drab, William Blair: Queried on overtime category and OpEx forecast. Thames said, "the way we're thinking about this right now is that we actually saw a really nice backlog build in overtime projects... our engineering workload is really at an all-time high." Drab also sought clarity on guidance range caution, to which Thames replied, "we are being more cautious in really the demand side of the equation, just given the uncertainty that we see, and our customers are seeing in the trade environment."
  • Justin Ages, CJS Securities: Asked about capital allocation. Schott responded, "we have our capital investments for growth...with all of the technology investments that we have going in, that's about 1% for next year." On M&A, "we have a lot of tailwinds at our back, really looking -- hoping to execute something in the near term on M&A."
  • Jon Braatz, Kansas City Capital: Sought clarification on net tariff impact. Thames said, "on a gross basis, we gave a range of $16 million to $20 million. And we believe on a net impact, it is somewhere in the $4 million to $6 million range within the current fiscal year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a slightly cautious but constructive tone, repeatedly pressing for details on tariff impacts, margin headwinds, and sustainability of backlog and guidance. Questions reflected concern about macro uncertainty and the potential for trade disruption.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone in prepared remarks, acknowledging challenges but emphasizing mitigation strategies and resilience. In Q&A, Thames stated, "we are being more cautious...given the uncertainty," showing a shift to prudent optimism versus previous quarters.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained cautious, with heightened focus on tariff risks. Management's sentiment shifted from confident optimism to a more measured approach due to external uncertainties, while reiterating confidence in strategic initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Fiscal 2026 revenue guidance was expanded to $495 million to $535 million versus the prior quarter’s fiscal 2025 guidance of $495 million to $515 million. Adjusted EBITDA guidance is essentially flat at the midpoint versus last quarter’s range.
  • Management’s language shifted from optimism about margin expansion to caution regarding near-term margin headwinds due to tariffs. The guidance now factors in expected tariff impacts, whereas previous guidance had not.
  • Analysts continued to focus on backlog strength and trade risks, but the current quarter saw more questions about the net impact of tariffs, capital allocation, and OpEx/CapEx mix.
  • Strategic focus remains on diversification, operational excellence, and capital discipline, but more emphasis is placed on supply chain resiliency and tariff mitigation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified tariffs as a significant challenge, estimating a gross impact of $16 million to $20 million and a net impact of $4 million to $6 million for fiscal 2026, primarily in the first half.
  • Thames outlined mitigation strategies: pricing actions, USMCA compliance, global footprint optimization, and supply chain reconfiguration. He noted "we are executing a multipronged strategy...to offset rising input costs while maintaining competitiveness and customer value."
  • Analysts highlighted concerns about macro uncertainty, trade disruptions, and the sustainability of improved order momentum, especially in key verticals like LNG and data centers.

Final Takeaway

Thermon Group enters fiscal 2026 with strong order momentum, a robust and diversified backlog, and confidence in its ability to offset tariff-driven cost headwinds through targeted pricing and operational initiatives. Management remains focused on capital discipline, new product development, and market expansion, while acknowledging the need for caution due to ongoing global trade uncertainties. The company’s capital allocation priorities and commitment to operational excellence support a resilient outlook amid market volatility.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Autodesk sets Q2 guidance above analysts estimates
Cal Redwood Acquisition prices 20M units in IPO
data.description:
• Cal Redwood Acquisition (CRA) announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 20M units at $10/unit on May 22, 2025.
  • The units are expected to be listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “CRAQU” beginning May 23, 2025.
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one right to receive one tenth of a Class A ordinary share upon the consummation of an initial business combination.
  • Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the Company expects that its Class A ordinary shares and rights will be listed on Nasdaq under the symbols “CRA” and “CRAQR,” respectively.
  • The offering is expected to close on May 27, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Onconetix receives additional notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Onconetix (NASDAQ:ONCO) said on Thursday that it has received a delisting letter from Nasdaq, indicating that the company’s failure to file its quarterly report is in violation of its continued listing requirements. 
  • The company has already received a deficiency notice from Nasdaq that the company was not in compliance, given its failure to timely file its annual report. 
  • The company intends to file the Form 10-K and 10-Q as promptly as possible to regain compliance.
